message:
Major TUI enhancements and coordinator improvements

- Add dialog system with confirmation and input dialogs
- Expand TUI model with account management features
- Add comprehensive test coverage for TUI components
- Enhance coordinator with better state management
- Add API endpoints for account operations
- Improve styles with consistent theming
- Add web interface scaffolding
- Track beads progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-7pi3","title":"Epic: Web Control Plane (Next.js 16 / React 19 / Tailwind 4)","description":"Purpose
- Provide a premium web UI for monitoring and managing caam across machines with modern web stack ergonomics.

Background
- Some workflows (multi-machine status, usage dashboards, bulk actions) are easier in a web UI than a terminal.
- Next.js 16 + React 19 + Tailwind 4 + Lucide + Framer Motion are the intended stack for a polished, agent-friendly UI.

Scope
- Local-only web server exposing caam status + actions with token-based auth.
- A Next.js 16 App Router frontend with a cohesive design system, animations, and accessible UI.
- Strong security posture and update policy for React/Next/Tailwind dependencies.
- Comprehensive unit + E2E tests and visual regression coverage.

Non-Goals
- Public hosting or multi-tenant SaaS (local-first only).

Success Criteria
- Users can view status, profiles, and distributed coordinator health from a browser with minimal setup.

 {"id":"caam-blxo","title":"Implement real-time session token tracking","description":"# Task: Implement real-time session token tracking

## Overview
Track token usage during an active session in real-time, not just from historical logs. This enables accurate burn rate calculation even before logs are written.

## Why This Matters
Historical log parsing is great for:
- Cost analysis
- Long-term trends
- Post-session review

But for LIVE prediction during a session:
- Logs may not be written yet (buffered)
- Want sub-second burn rate updates
- Need to detect usage spikes immediately

## Technical Details

### Session Tracker
```go
// internal/usage/session.go

type SessionTracker struct {
    mu          sync.RWMutex
    entries     []TokenEntry
    windowSize  time.Duration
    
    // Callbacks
    onUsage     func(entry TokenEntry)
}

type TokenEntry struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Model       string
    InputTokens int64
    OutputTokens int64
    Source      string  // "api_response", "log_parse", "stream_estimate"
}

func (t *SessionTracker) Record(entry TokenEntry)
func (t *SessionTracker) BurnRate(window time.Duration) *BurnRateInfo
func (t *SessionTracker) TotalTokens() int64
```

### Integration with SmartRunner
```go
func (r *SmartRunner) handleAPIResponse(resp *Response) {
    // Extract usage from API response headers or body
    if usage := extractUsage(resp); usage != nil {
        r.sessionTracker.Record(TokenEntry{
            Timestamp:    time.Now(),
            Model:        resp.Model,
            InputTokens:  usage.InputTokens,
            OutputTokens: usage.OutputTokens,
            Source:       "api_response",
        })
    }
}
```

### Stream Token Estimation
For streaming responses, estimate tokens in real-time:
```go
func (t *SessionTracker) EstimateFromStream(chunk string) {
    // Rough estimation: ~4 chars per token for English
    estimatedTokens := len(chunk) / 4
    
    t.Record(TokenEntry{
        Timestamp:    time.Now(),
        OutputTokens: int64(estimatedTokens),
        Source:       "stream_estimate",
    })
}
```

### Merging with Log Data
```go
func (t *SessionTracker) MergeLogData(entries []*logs.LogEntry) {
    // Deduplicate based on timestamp/conversation_id
    // Prefer log data over estimates when available
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tracks tokens from API responses in real-time
- [ ] Estimates tokens from streaming output
- [ ] Calculates accurate burn rate from live data
- [ ] Merges with historical log data correctly
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent access
- [ ] Memory-efficient (rolling window, not unbounded)

## Test Cases
1. Record and retrieve token entries
2. Calculate burn rate from entries
3. Stream estimation accuracy
4. Merge with log data (deduplication)
5. Thread safety under concurrent writes

## Files to Create
- internal/usage/session.go
- internal/usage/session_test.go

## Dependencies
- Create BurnRateInfo struct (caam-ef8r)

 {"id":"caam-bxq","title":"Enhanced init wizard with auth discovery","description":"## Overview
Transform the first-run experience from "read docs, run 5 commands" to "run one command, answer prompts, done." The init wizard discovers existing auth files, extracts identities, and guides users through setup.

## The Problem
Current first-time setup requires:
1. Read README to understand concepts
2. Ensure you're logged into each tool
3. Run `caam backup claude <email>` (must know email)
4. Repeat for each tool and account
5. Optionally configure shell integration

This takes 10+ minutes and requires understanding caam's mental model first.

## The Solution

## Technical Design

### Auth File Discovery
Scan standard locations for each provider:
```go
func DiscoverAuth(tool string) (*DiscoveredAuth, error) {
    spec := provider.Registry.Get(tool).AuthFiles()
    for _, f := range spec {
        if exists(f.Path) {
            identity, err := extractIdentity(tool, f.Path)
            return &DiscoveredAuth{
                Tool:     tool,
                Path:     f.Path,
                Identity: identity, // email or account ID
                Valid:    err == nil,
            }, nil
        }
    }
    return nil, ErrNoAuth
}
```

### Identity Extraction
Parse auth files to extract user identity:

**Claude** (`~/.claude.json`):
```json
{
  "oauthAccessToken": "...",
  "oauthRefreshToken": "...",
  "claudeAiSessionKey": "sk-ant-...",
  // Look for email in JWT claims or additional fields
}
```
- Decode JWT if present, extract email/sub claim
- Fall back to "unknown" if can't parse

**Codex** (`~/.codex/auth.json`):
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "user": {
    "email": "dev@company.com",
    "name": "Developer"
  }
}
```
- Direct field access

**Gemini** (`~/.gemini/settings.json`):
```json
{
  "account": {
    "email": "user@gmail.com"
  }
}
```
- Direct field access

### Wizard Flow Controller
```go
type WizardState struct {
    Discovered   []DiscoveredAuth
    SavedProfiles []string
    AddedAccounts []string
    ShellSetup   bool
}

func RunWizard() error {
    state := &WizardState{}
    
    // Phase 1: Discovery
    state.Discovered = discoverAllAuth()
    showDiscoveryResults(state.Discovered)
    
    // Phase 2: Save existing
    for _, auth := range state.Discovered {
        if promptSave(auth) {
            name := promptProfileName(auth.Identity)
            vault.Backup(auth.Tool, name)
            state.SavedProfiles = append(state.SavedProfiles, name)
        }
    }
    
    // Phase 3: Add more (optional loop)
    for {
        if !promptAddMore() {
            break
        }
        runAddAccountFlow()
    }
    
    // Phase 4: Shell integration
    if promptShellIntegration() {
        installShellInit()
        state.ShellSetup = true
    }
    
    // Phase 5: Summary
    showSummary(state)
    return nil
}
```

### Interactive Prompts
Using bubbletea or simple stdin for prompts:
- Yes/No prompts with smart defaults
- Text input with default values
- Progress indicators during operations
- Clear section headers

## Configuration Created
After wizard completes:
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
version: 1
defaults:
  claude: personal
  codex: work
shell:
  auto_aliases: true
  completion: true
```

## Edge Cases

### No auth files found

```
  [2] Log in to Codex now  
  [3] Log in to Gemini now
```

### Existing vault profiles

```
  codex: main

  [F]resh - backup existing, start over

Choice [M]:
```


## Flags
```bash
caam init              # Interactive wizard
caam init --quick      # Auto-save all discovered, skip prompts
caam init --shell=zsh  # Pre-specify shell type
caam init --no-shell   # Skip shell integration step
caam init --reset      # Clear existing and start fresh
```

## Implementation Tasks
1. **Auth discovery** (internal/discovery/scanner.go)
   - Scan auth file locations
   - Check file existence and readability

2. **Identity extraction** (internal/discovery/identity.go)
   - Parse each provider's auth format
   - JWT decoding for tokens
   - Graceful fallback on parse failure

3. **Wizard UI** (cmd/caam/cmd/init.go)
   - Section-based flow
   - Interactive prompts
   - Progress feedback

4. **Shell integration installer** (cmd/caam/cmd/shell_init.go)
   - Detect shell type
   - Append to rc file
   - Idempotent (don't duplicate)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Discovers auth for all three providers
- [ ] Extracts identity (email) when possible
- [ ] Saves discovered sessions as profiles
- [ ] Guides through adding additional accounts
- [ ] Installs shell integration optionally
- [ ] Works with no existing auth (guides to first login)
- [ ] Handles existing profiles gracefully
- [ ] Completes in <2 minutes for typical user

## Dependencies
- Vault.Backup() for saving profiles
- Provider auth file specs
- JWT parsing for identity extraction
- Shell init infrastructure (caam-k9z)

 {"id":"caam-by4","title":"Terminal UI (TUI) with Bubble Tea","description":"## Overview
A visual terminal interface for managing caam profiles. While the CLI is fully functional, a TUI provides:
- At-a-glance view of all profiles across providers
- Visual feedback on status (logged in, locked, active)
- Discoverable interface for new users
- Running session management

## Design (from original spec)
Layout:
- Left panel: Provider list (Codex / Claude / Gemini)
- Center panel: Profiles table (Name, Auth mode, Logged-in?, Last used, Notes)
- Right panel: Action pane + detail view for selected profile

Keybindings:
- ↑/↓: Select profile
- Enter: Run default command (launch CLI)
- l: Login/refresh auth
- o: Open account page in browser
- e: Edit profile (notes, browser config)
- d: Delete profile (with confirmation)
- /: Search/filter profiles
- Tab: Switch between providers
- q: Quit

## Implementation
Use Bubble Tea (bubbletea) with:
- bubbles for standard components (list, table, textinput)
- lipgloss for styling

## User Value
- Visual overview prevents mistakes (wrong account)
- Running sessions visible prevents data corruption
- Lower learning curve than CLI-only

## Consideration
TUI should be the default when running 'caam' with no args, but all functionality must remain available via CLI for scripting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:51:16.807075968Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T06:46:22.843793337Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T06:46:22.843793337Z","close_reason":"All TUI components implemented: scaffolding (caam-qpx), provider panel (caam-ta7), profiles table (caam-nmm), detail panel (caam-f9h), keyboard navigation (caam-j14). Rich ASCII table for interactive terminal use:


### Brief Renderer
One-line summary for tmux/status bar:

### JSON Renderer
Machine-readable for scripting:

 {"id":"caam-ewh","title":"Smart profile rotation","description":"Intelligently select profiles to vary usage patterns and reduce detectability.

## The Problem
If users always activate profiles in the same order (A → B → C → A...), this creates a detectable pattern. Varying the selection makes usage look more organic.

## Solution
Multi-factor scoring algorithm for profile selection:

```go
func ScoreProfile(profile, allProfiles, config) float64 {
    score := 0.0
    
    // Factor 1: Health (prefer fresh tokens)
    if config.PreferHealthy {
        health := getProfileHealth(profile)
        if health.TokenExpiresAt.After(time.Now().Add(24*time.Hour)) {
            score += 100  // Very healthy
        } else if health.TokenExpiresAt.After(time.Now()) {
            score += 50   // Healthy but expiring soon
        }
        // Expired = score 0
    }
    
    // Factor 2: Cooldown (exclude if in cooldown)
    if isInCooldown(profile) {
        score -= 10000  // Effectively excluded
    }
    
    // Factor 3: Recency (penalize recently used)
    if config.AvoidRecentHours > 0 {
        lastUsed := getLastActivation(profile)
        hoursSince := time.Since(lastUsed).Hours()
        if hoursSince < config.AvoidRecentHours {
            score -= (config.AvoidRecentHours - hoursSince) * 10
        }
    }
    
    // Factor 4: Randomness (break ties unpredictably)
    score += rand.Float64() * config.Randomness * 100
    
    return score
}
```

## Usage
```bash
# Auto-select best profile based on rotation algorithm
caam activate claude --next
# or
caam activate claude  # Uses rotation if no default set

# Output:
# Selected claude/personal-account (score: 142.3)
#   ✓ Healthy token (expires in 5 days)
#   ✓ Not in cooldown
#   ✓ Last used 8 hours ago
# Activated claude profile 'personal-account'

# See rotation scores for all profiles
caam rotate status claude
# Output:
# claude profiles (sorted by rotation score):
#   personal-account: 142.3 ✓ healthy, last used 8h ago
#   work-account:     89.1  ⚠ token expires in 2h, last used 1h ago
#   backup:          -9900  ✗ in cooldown (3h remaining)
```

## When Rotation is Used
1. `caam activate <tool> --next` - Explicit rotation
2. `caam activate <tool>` with no profile AND no default set
3. `caam activate <tool>` when default profile is in cooldown

## Integration with Other Features
- Uses health data from health subsystem
- Uses cooldown data from cooldown tracking
- Uses activation history from activity database
- Respects stealth config settings

## Files
- internal/stealth/rotation.go: Scoring algorithm
- internal/stealth/rotation_test.go: Tests
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Integration

## Purpose
Intelligently select which profile to activate based on multiple factors,

Changed `--next` to `--auto`

Rationale: `--next` implies sequential/round-robin which is just ONE algorithm.

```
  - No recent limit hits
```

- **round_robin**: Simple sequential rotation
- **random**: Random selection (least predictable but may cluster)

 {"id":"caam-f66e","title":"EPIC: Cost Dashboard & Token Analytics","description":"# EPIC: Cost Dashboard

## Strategic Context

Users with $200+/month AI subscriptions want to know:
1. How much value they're getting from their subscriptions
2. Token consumption patterns
3. What they would pay if using pay-per-token plans
4. Cost breakdown by model (Opus is more expensive)

CodexBar provides this for display; caam should provide it too, but also USE this data for smarter decisions.

## Background

### Pay-Per-Token Pricing (as of 2025)
Claude API pricing:
- claude-3-opus: $15/M input, $75/M output
- claude-3-sonnet: $3/M input, $15/M output
- claude-3-haiku: $0.25/M input, $1.25/M output
- Cache read: 90% discount on input
- Cache creation: 25% premium on input

OpenAI pricing:
- gpt-4o: $5/M input, $15/M output
- gpt-4o-mini: $0.15/M input, $0.60/M output

### Why This Matters for caam

If a user with 2 Claude Max accounts ($400/mo) sees they're consuming $600/mo equivalent in tokens, they're getting value. If they're only consuming $50/mo equivalent, maybe they should downgrade.

Also: Model-specific costs help decide whether to push Opus-heavy workloads to accounts with fresh Opus limits.

## Implementation

 {"id":"caam-ldxo","title":"Epic: Installation, Updates, and Dependency Bootstrap","description":"Purpose
- Make caam installs, updates, and dependency management bulletproof, idempotent, and user-friendly across macOS/Linux, including multi-machine setups.

Background
- Current install.sh fetches GitHub releases but does not verify checksums or signatures, has limited cache-busting, and lacks a built-in self-update command.
- Local repos (repo_updater, xf) already demonstrate stronger patterns: checksum verification, cache-busting, optional gum UI, and interactive confirmations.
- Recent tooling ecosystem trends emphasize signed release artifacts and explicit verification (e.g., cosign-verified checksums in Charm tools).

Scope
- Harden install.sh with checksum/signature verification, cache-busting, and safe fallbacks.
- Add caam update/self-update workflows with atomic replace and rollback.
- Provide dependency bootstrap (gum, wezterm, tailscale, playwright, gh, jq) with safe prompts and non-interactive mode.
- Support auto-update scheduling (opt-in) and distributed update orchestration.
- Add comprehensive installer/update tests and logging.

Non-Goals
- Shipping a package manager; use system package managers when possible.

Success Criteria
- Installs verify integrity, upgrades are idempotent, and missing dependencies are handled gracefully.
- Updates and dependency bootstrap are auditable, reversible, and do not surprise users.

- Make the installer safer, clearer, and more reliable while remaining a copy/paste one-liner.

Background
- Local installers (repo_updater, xf) show best practices: cache-busting, gum-enhanced output, non-interactive mode, and checksum verification.

Details

- Add cache-busting query param in URLs (opt-out env var).
- Add gum-based formatting if gum is installed and stdout is a TTY; graceful fallback otherwise.
- Add NO_COLOR + CI/non-interactive behavior (no prompts, deterministic exit codes).
- Add explicit --verify option (run caam --version and optional self-test).
- Add explicit --channel=stable|beta and --version override.
- Ensure install is idempotent: if the same version is installed, skip download unless --force.
- Preserve previous binary as backup and restore if replace fails.
- Improve error messages with actionable guidance (missing curl/wget, PATH, permissions).

Tests
- Shell tests for: checksum failure, signature failure, gum present/absent, non-tty, cache-bust on/off, version already installed, rollback on partial failure.
- Mock GitHub API responses to validate parsing and fallback behavior.

Success
- Installer is safe, audit-friendly, and pleasant to read in the terminal.

+
+// CommandAction represents an executable command in the palette.
+type CommandAction struct {
+	Name        string
+	Description string
+	Shortcut    string
+	Action      string // Action identifier for handling
+}
+
+// CommandPaletteDialog is a searchable command palette overlay.
+type CommandPaletteDialog struct {
+	title    string
+	input    textinput.Model
+	commands []CommandAction
+	filtered []CommandAction
+	selected int
+	result   DialogResult
+	chosen   *CommandAction
+	keys     DialogKeyMap
+	styles   Styles
+	width    int
+	height   int
+	focused  bool
+}
+
+// NewCommandPaletteDialog creates a new command palette dialog.
+func NewCommandPaletteDialog(title string, commands []CommandAction) *CommandPaletteDialog {
+	ti := textinput.New()
+	ti.Focus()
+	ti.CharLimit = 64
+	ti.Width = 40
+	ti.Placeholder = "Type to filter commands..."
+
+	return &CommandPaletteDialog{
+		title:    title,
+		input:    ti,
+		commands: commands,
+		filtered: commands,
+		selected: 0,
+		result:   DialogResultNone,
+		keys:     DefaultDialogKeyMap(),
+		styles:   DefaultStyles(),
+		width:    50,
+		height:   20,
+		focused:  true,
+	}
+}
+
+// DefaultCommands returns the default set of palette commands.
+func DefaultCommands() []CommandAction {
+	return []CommandAction{
+		{Name: "Activate Profile", Description: "Switch to the selected profile", Shortcut: "enter", Action: "activate"},
+		{Name: "Backup Current Auth", Description: "Create a backup of current auth", Shortcut: "b", Action: "backup"},
+		{Name: "Delete Profile", Description: "Delete the selected profile", Shortcut: "d", Action: "delete"},
+		{Name: "Edit Profile", Description: "Edit profile details", Shortcut: "e", Action: "edit"},
+		{Name: "Login/Refresh", Description: "Refresh authentication", Shortcut: "l", Action: "login"},
+		{Name: "Open in Browser", Description: "Open provider in browser", Shortcut: "o", Action: "open"},
+		{Name: "Set Project Association", Description: "Link profile to current project", Shortcut: "p", Action: "project"},
+		{Name: "Usage Statistics", Description: "View usage stats", Shortcut: "u", Action: "usage"},
+		{Name: "Sync Pool", Description: "Manage sync pool", Shortcut: "S", Action: "sync"},
+		{Name: "Export Vault", Description: "Export profiles to vault", Shortcut: "E", Action: "export"},
+		{Name: "Import Bundle", Description: "Import profiles from bundle", Shortcut: "I", Action: "import"},
+		{Name: "Help", Description: "Show help screen", Shortcut: "?", Action: "help"},
+	}
+}
+
+// SetStyles sets the styles for the dialog.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) SetStyles(styles Styles) {
+	d.styles = styles
+	d.input.Cursor.Style = styles.InputCursor
+}
+
+// Focus focuses the dialog.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) Focus() {
+	d.focused = true
+	d.input.Focus()
+}
+
+// Blur blurs the dialog.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) Blur() {
+	d.focused = false
+	d.input.Blur()
+}
+
+// Result returns the dialog result.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) Result() DialogResult {
+	return d.result
+}
+
+// ChosenCommand returns the selected command (if any).
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) ChosenCommand() *CommandAction {
+	return d.chosen
+}
+
+// Reset resets the dialog to its initial state.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) Reset() {
+	d.input.Reset()
+	d.result = DialogResultNone
+	d.chosen = nil
+	d.selected = 0
+	d.filtered = d.commands
+	d.input.Focus()
+}
+
+// SetWidth sets the dialog width.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) SetWidth(width int) {
+	d.width = width
+	d.input.Width = width - 8
+}
+
+// filterCommands filters commands based on the current input.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) filterCommands() {
+	query := strings.ToLower(d.input.Value())
+	if query == "" {
+		d.filtered = d.commands
+		return
+	}
+
+	var filtered []CommandAction
+	for _, cmd := range d.commands {
+		name := strings.ToLower(cmd.Name)
+		desc := strings.ToLower(cmd.Description)
+		shortcut := strings.ToLower(cmd.Shortcut)
+		if strings.Contains(name, query) ||
+			strings.Contains(desc, query) ||
+			strings.Contains(shortcut, query) {
+			filtered = append(filtered, cmd)
+		}
+	}
+	d.filtered = filtered
+
+	// Reset selection if out of bounds
+	if d.selected >= len(d.filtered) {
+		d.selected = 0
+	}
+}
+
+// Update handles messages for the dialog.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) Update(msg tea.Msg) (*CommandPaletteDialog, tea.Cmd) {
+	if !d.focused {
+		return d, nil
+	}
+
+	switch msg := msg.(type) {
+	case tea.KeyMsg:
+		switch msg.Type {
+		case tea.KeyEscape:
+			d.result = DialogResultCancel
+			return d, nil
+		case tea.KeyEnter:
+			if len(d.filtered) > 0 && d.selected < len(d.filtered) {
+				d.chosen = &d.filtered[d.selected]
+				d.result = DialogResultSubmit
+			}
+			return d, nil
+		case tea.KeyUp, tea.KeyCtrlP:
+			if d.selected > 0 {
+				d.selected--
+			}
+			return d, nil
+		case tea.KeyDown, tea.KeyCtrlN:
+			if d.selected < len(d.filtered)-1 {
+				d.selected++
+			}
+			return d, nil
+		}
+	}
+
+	// Update input and filter
+	var cmd tea.Cmd
+	d.input, cmd = d.input.Update(msg)
+	d.filterCommands()
+	return d, cmd
+}
+
+// View renders the dialog.
+func (d *CommandPaletteDialog) View() string {
+	var content strings.Builder
+
+	// Title
+	if d.title != "" {
+		content.WriteString(d.styles.DialogTitle.Render(d.title))
+		content.WriteString("\n\n")
+	}
+
+	// Search input
+	content.WriteString(d.input.View())
+	content.WriteString("\n\n")
+
+	// Command list
+	maxVisible := 8
+	if len(d.filtered) < maxVisible {
+		maxVisible = len(d.filtered)
+	}
+
+	// Calculate scroll offset
+	scrollOffset := 0
+	if d.selected >= maxVisible {
+		scrollOffset = d.selected - maxVisible + 1
+	}
+
+	for i := scrollOffset; i < scrollOffset+maxVisible && i < len(d.filtered); i++ {
+		cmd := d.filtered[i]
+		isSelected := i == d.selected
+
+		// Format: [shortcut] Name - Description
+		shortcutStyle := d.styles.StatusKey
+		nameStyle := d.styles.Item
+		if isSelected {
+			nameStyle = d.styles.SelectedItem
+		}
+
+		shortcut := shortcutStyle.Render("[" + cmd.Shortcut + "]")
+		name := nameStyle.Render(" " + cmd.Name)
+		desc := d.styles.StatusText.Render(" - " + cmd.Description)
+
+		line := shortcut + name + desc
+		if isSelected {
+			line = "▸ " + line
+		} else {
+			line = "  " + line

+name: CI
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+    paths:
+      - "web/dashboard/**"
+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main]
+    paths:
+      - "web/dashboard/**"
+
+defaults:
+  run:
+    working-directory: web/dashboard
+
+jobs:
+  build:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Setup pnpm
+        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
+        with:
+          version: 10
+
+      - name: Setup Node.js
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+          cache: "pnpm"
+          cache-dependency-path: "web/dashboard/pnpm-lock.yaml"
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
+
+      - name: Type check
+        run: pnpm typecheck
+
+      - name: Lint
+        run: pnpm lint
+
+      - name: Build
+        run: pnpm build
+
+  test:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    needs: build
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Setup pnpm
+        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
+        with:
+          version: 10
+
+      - name: Setup Node.js
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+          cache: "pnpm"
+          cache-dependency-path: "web/dashboard/pnpm-lock.yaml"
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
+
+      - name: Run unit tests
+        run: pnpm test
+
+  e2e:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    needs: build
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Setup pnpm
+        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
+        with:
+          version: 10
+
+      - name: Setup Node.js
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+          cache: "pnpm"
+          cache-dependency-path: "web/dashboard/pnpm-lock.yaml"
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
+
+      - name: Install Playwright browsers
+        run: pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium
+
+      - name: Run E2E tests
+        run: pnpm test:e2e
